UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, ORAL ARGUMENTS ARE HEARD FROM BOTH SIDES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT NASIR JAVAID, DEJAUNA JOSEPH MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN/SHIFT BURDEN AND SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION 1 THOUGH 5 ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. SEE WANG V. WAL-MART REAL ESTATE BUSINESS TRUST (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 790; PERSONAL COURT REPORTERS V. RAND (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 182. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 128.5. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED, NO SANCTIONS. JUDGE A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK B. PAREDES, REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO, CSR.5229,(501)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Poe, Gaw Poe,LLP 415-745-33018 for defendant and moving party, Nasir Javaid
No appearances by plaintff.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANTs NASIR JAVAID and DEJAUNA JOSEPH's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED. Defendants' reading and interpretation of the contractual provisions are not supported by the language of the contract. Specifically, defendants' suggestion that section 5(c) was operative only up to August 31, 2005 ("Entitlement Deadline") (See Reply 6:5-12), renders this section's condition precedent provision "if Purchaser has not terminated this Agreement" meaningless as Purchaser's right to terminate the Agreement did not ripen until August 21, 2005 ("If (1) Purchaser does not obtain the Entitlement on or before August 31, 2005 (the "Entitlement Deadline")', then"). Moving party to submit proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) = (501/AJR/rg)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristen Drake, Esq. of Wood Robins LLP (415-247-7900), attorney for plaintiff/opposing party
Lucia MacDonald, Esq. of Mode Law (415-230-5395), attorney for defendant/moving party

Prior to UD Motion calendar call the tentative ruling is read to counsel for both sides by legal research assistant, Olga Grecova.

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court rules now as follows: Tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS NASIR JAVAID and DEJAUNA JOSEPH's Motion For Order Transferring Plaintitf'S 6th Cause Of Action To Appropriate Court is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that venue may be changed for a single cause of action. Jhirmack Enterprises v. Sup. Ct. (1979) 96 Cal.App.3d 715; Capp Care, Inc v. Sup. Ct. (1987) 195 Cal.App.3d 507; Ah Fong v. Sternes (1889) 79 Cal.30 are not on point and are cited for the first time in reply. Additionally, motion per CCP 392 is untimely under CCP 396b(a) where Demurrer, Special Motion to Strike and Answer have been filed. Defendants fail to provide sufficient evidence as to how witnesses will be inconvenienced by trial held in San Francisco.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $3,785.00 to be paid by Defendants' counsel within 10 days of notice of entry of order.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Angelica Gutierrez, CSR No. 13292, Bus. Tel: 415-433-5777. (D501)

Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Attorney Timothy Martin Flaherty (415)984-8507 for Nasir Javaid the moving party/ Attorney Gregory J. Wood (415)247-7900 for Joseph Tierney the opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties in the matter of DEFENDANTS NASIR JAVAID and DEJAUNA JOSEPH's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, the Court's tentative Ruling Stands as follows; SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part: Demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled. Existence and breach of a valid bargained-for contract is sufficiently alleged. Plaintiff's timely performance is sufficiently alleged. Approval of entitlements date is an issue of fact not properly raised on demurrer.
Demurer to the second cause of action for negligence is overruled. Negligence based on tenancy in common relationship is sufficiently alleged.
Demurer to the third and fourth causes of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege independent unjustified wrongful act and non-speculative beneficial relationship with third parties.
Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action.
Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to all causes of action re: 376 Castro Street, LLC to allege facts showing standing. Order signed and filed in open court. Judge Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk J. Rumsey, Not Reported. (501)
